name: Marla Gibbs' Carrot Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a large bowl, beat together raw sugar and eggs until well combined.
3. Add vegetable oil to the bowl and mix in thoroughly.
4. In a separate bowl, sift together flour, cinnamon, baking powder, and salt.
5. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, stirring until just combined.
6. Stir in grated carrots, crushed pineapple, and chopped pecans.
7. Divide the batter evenly between two greased and waxed paper-lined 9-inch round cake pans.
8. Bake for 35-40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
9. Allow cakes to cool in the pans for about 10 minutes before removing them completely.
10. Cool the cakes on wire racks.
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat together softened cream cheese (8 oz), butter, and vanilla extract until smooth.
2. Gradually add powdered sugar, sifting it in, and continue beating until the frosting is light and fluffy.
3. Spread half of the frosting evenly over the top of one cake layer.
4. Place the second cake layer on top and spread the remaining frosting over the top and sides of the cake.
